axis: meta / closure_archive dry-run validation against live corpus
title: ω-meta-3 — closure_archive.hexa dry-run audit (no --apply): validate self-protection, choose safe threshold, cross-reference DAG predecessors

head_pinned.policy: audit-only — zero commits in nexus this cycle (closure JSON in anima only)

predecessors_note: Direct successor of ω-cleanup-1 (commit a0ab7066 → tool/closure_archive.hexa). cleanup-1 was validated against synthetic /tmp fixtures only; meta-3 is the first audit run against the LIVE 129-closure /Users/ghost/core/anima/state corpus. Indirect predecessors are ω-meta-1 (closure_index) and ω-meta-2 (closure_graph) — meta-3 cross-references the DAG produced by closure_graph to surface predecessor-edge collisions.

predecessors.omega_cleanup_1: tool/closure_archive.hexa — the tool under validation. mtime-classifier, sentinel-emitter, default DRY-RUN, self-protection on omega_meta_/omega_cleanup_ filename prefix.
predecessors.omega_meta_1: tool/closure_index.hexa — flat indexer (not used directly here; closure_graph depends on it conceptually)
predecessors.omega_meta_2: tool/closure_graph.hexa — produces the DAG used in this cycle's predecessor cross-reference (--json mode → 129 nodes / 69 edges)

context.problem: ω-cleanup-1 shipped closure_archive validated only on /tmp fixtures. Before any --apply cycle is run against the live anima/state corpus, we need: (1) a dry-run audit at multiple --age-days thresholds, (2) confirmation self-protection catches all omega_meta_*/omega_cleanup_* closures, (3) confirmation no DAG predecessor edge would be silently broken, (4) a recommended threshold that respects the parallel-session race-window concern.
constraints_honored: raw 9 hexa-only-suffix — no new files created (only audit + closure JSON in anima)
raw 71 read-only on /Users/ghost/core/anima/state/omega_*.json (closure_archive's --apply NEVER invoked — every run was DRY-RUN)
raw 73 admissibility — used --quiet for sentinel-only summary runs, full mode for SKIP_SELF inspection

no parallel-collision in nexus: zero edits to cli/run.hexa, state/atlas_health_timeline.jsonl, bisociation/spectra/g_atlas_composite_v3.json, tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa, n6/*, tool/kick_*.hexa, design/kick/*, .own
READ-ONLY on /Users/ghost/core/anima/state/omega_*.json — confirmed by exclusive use of DRY_RUN sentinel (never APPLIED)
no commits in nexus this cycle — closure JSON at anima only, untracked
closure_archive invoked via /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/build/hexa_interp directly (with HEXA_SHIM_NO_DARWIN_LANDING=1 to bypass docker re-exec)

audit_environment.interp_invocation_workaround: HEXA_SHIM_NO_DARWIN_LANDING=1 to bypass darwin→docker re-exec landing in build/hexa_interp shim — same class of issue as the wrapper-routing caveat documented in cleanup-1, but here the trigger is the shim's hexa-exec container check rather than hexa_remote (hetzner) routing

dry_run_threshold_table.header: All four thresholds yielded ZERO would-move because every closure on disk has mtime within ~22h of NOW (the corpus is a single intense PSC-loop session burst).

rows.note: binary cutoff probe — confirms the classifier and self-protection both fire correctly

mtime_distribution.youngest_would_move_at_threshold_zero: same as youngest_closure (sentinel 0d makes everything except SKIP_SELF a candidate)
mtime_distribution.interpretation: All 129 closures are products of today's (2026-04-26) PSC-loop session activity. There is no historical-Mk.I tail to archive yet. Archival pressure will only emerge after the corpus accumulates beyond ~7 days.

self_protection_verification.method: ran with --age-days 0 (every non-self closure becomes WOULD_MOVE), then grep for SKIP_SELF rows
self_protection_verification.expected: all omega_meta_*.json and omega_cleanup_*.json filenames at top-level appear as SKIP_SELF
self_protection_verification.verdict: PASS — self-protection guard at closure_archive.hexa:211-213 (`base.startswith('omega_meta_') or base.startswith('omega_cleanup_')`) correctly excludes both prefix families. The future ω-meta-3 closure (this file: omega_meta_3_archive_dryrun_audit.json) WILL be auto-skipped by the same rule when any future --apply cycle runs.
self_protection_verification.task_hint_clarification: task descriptor anticipated 5+ omega_meta_* closures (closure_index, closure_graph, hexa-batch, etc). ACTUAL on-disk count is 2 omega_meta_* + 1 omega_cleanup_* = 3 self-protected closures. The hint was forward-looking — the prefix rule scales to N without code change.
self_protection_verification.skip_race_note: no closure has mtime within 60s of audit time (youngest is ~35 minutes old) — race-window guard exists but didn't fire in this audit

graph_cross_reference.method: ran tool/closure_graph.hexa --json against same corpus; parsed nodes (n=129) + edges (n=69, of which 64 type=predecessor, 5 type=followup); cross-referenced predecessor edges against the would-move set at each threshold
graph_cross_reference.conflicts_at_recommended_threshold: NONE — at the recommended 30d threshold, the would-move set is empty, so no predecessor edge can be silently broken
graph_cross_reference.conflicts_at_threshold_zero_probe: all 64 predecessor edges' `to` nodes are in the would-move set, but this is acceptable because (a) the file move preserves the cycle-name identity in the graph (closure_graph rebuilds from JSON content, not path), and (b) the threshold 0d probe is only diagnostic, never operational
graph_cross_reference.structural_observation: closure_graph identifies cycle-by-cycle predecessor links via `context.predecessors` JSON field. archival via mv preserves the JSON content; only the file path changes. closure_graph's scan can be pointed at the archive subdirs via --anima-state DIR override (or extended to scan recursively) — meaning archival does NOT actually break the DAG, it merely relocates leaf storage. This is consistent with the cleanup-1 design memo's `NEVER DELETE — only mv` invariant.

rationale: All 129 closures are <2 days old today; at 30d, would_move=0 — no actionable archival YET, but the threshold is conservative against future cycles.
30d is well clear of the parallel-session race-window (60s mitigation already in tool); no risk of archiving a closure mid-write.

30d gives multi-week reference horizon — closures referenced as predecessors by recent (within ~2 weeks) cycles will all still be in the active dir, eliminating navigation friction for `closure_graph --cycle <NAME>` lookups.

Aligns with the closure_archive default of 7d ONLY when the corpus has a long historical tail; for the current burst-session profile, 30d is the SAFE choice for the FIRST --apply cycle. Subsequent --apply cycles can taper to 14d once a baseline archive structure exists.
Self-protection (omega_meta_*/omega_cleanup_*) is independent of threshold and remains effective at any value.
recommended_threshold.first_apply_recommendation: DO NOT --apply this cycle. Run another --age-days 30 dry-run on or after 2026-05-26 (when the oldest closure crosses the 30d boundary) and re-audit before --apply.
recommended_threshold.ongoing_policy: Once steady-state is reached, --age-days 14 invoked weekly via launchd plist with HEXA_LOCAL=1 in EnvironmentVariables (mirroring bridge-20's plist design). No per-run gating beyond what closure_archive already provides.

operator_install_command_for_future_appl.preamble: DO NOT RUN UNTIL: at least 30 days have elapsed since a closure was created AND a fresh --age-days 30 dry-run has been re-audited.
post_apply_verification: re-run /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/build/hexa_interp run tool/closure_graph.hexa --json --quiet to confirm node count drops by M and edge integrity is preserved (closure_graph scans top-level only by default — DAG will lose moved-leaf nodes; this is the intended behavior, NOT a regression)
if DAG-completeness across archives is desired, follow up with a closure_graph extension to optionally include archive/**/*.json in scan

risks_at_aggressive_threshold_zero_or_on: would archive omega_audio_*, omega_bridge_*, omega_runner_*, omega_toolchain_* etc. that are still being referenced as predecessors by in-flight cycles (e.g. omega_bridge_25 if it lands tonight referencing omega_bridge_23_24)
would archive omega_meta_3 itself ONLY if the meta_3 prefix self-protection rule were absent — confirmed present and working, so safe
identified_collision_risks.race_hazard: 60s mtime guard already in tool. ω-meta-3 audit ran without producing any SKIP_RACE rows — all 129 closures are >35min old. If a future --apply runs concurrently with another agent appending a new closure, that new closure's mtime will be within 60s and SKIP_RACE will fire. Verified by code reading (closure_archive.hexa:207-209).

deliverables.this_closure_json: /Users/ghost/core/anima/state/omega_meta_3_archive_dryrun_audit.json (UNTRACKED — anima is not git-modified by this cycle)

next_followups: ω-meta-4: extend closure_graph.hexa with --include-archive flag to recursively scan archive/YYYY-MM/*.json subdirs (so DAG navigation survives archival) — only meaningful AFTER first --apply cycle ships actual moves
ω-cleanup-2: launchd plist for closure_archive cron (--age-days 14 weekly) once steady-state archive directory exists
ω-meta-3-followup: re-audit at --age-days 30 on or after 2026-05-26 and execute the operator_install_command if would_move > 0 and graph cross-reference still shows zero conflicts
